subject: UX & Research
title: The Target Data Breach
description: A 60-second lesson on The Target Data Breach.

hook.text: In 2013, hackers stole 40 million credit card numbers from Target. Security alerts were triggered but ignored.

buildup.text: Target's security system detected the breach and sent alerts. The dashboard showed warnings in red.

discovery.text: But the dashboard showed hundreds of alerts daily. Real threats were buried in a sea of false positives.

twist.text: Security analysts suffered alert fatigue. They'd learned to ignore most warnings because most were harmless.

climax.text: Too many alerts desensitize users. Critical warnings must be rare and distinct to trigger real action.

punchline.text: Too many alerts train users to ignore all of them.

quiz.question: Why did Target's security team miss the breach alerts?
options: Alert fatigue from hundreds of daily warnings buried real threats in noise
The alerts were sent to the wrong email address
The security software wasn't installed properly
